hypothesis: In lanthanide and actinide-derived metal superhydrides (e.g., La, Ce, Th), introducing non-integer hydrogen stoichiometries that create partially occupied or mixed-sized hydrogen cages (x between 9.5 and 10.5) in Sm-type or double-hexagonal-close-packed metal lattices will increase the electron–phonon coupling constant λ by at least 20% and raise the superconducting Tc by more than 20 K at 200 GPa relative to the nearest integer MH10 analogue.
